commit.message:
Add level transition system with progressive maps

After clearing 5 waves, level completion triggers with stats screen.
Players can advance to the next level (keeping weapons/XP/level) or
restart from scratch. Each level scales enemy HP and damage by 15%.
Wave spawns also scale per level. Two buttons on completion screen:
NEXT LEVEL (continue) and RESTART (fresh start).
Fixes #187

commit.message:
Fix flaky DebouncerFixture tests on Windows CI

Increase debounce duration from 50ms to 200ms and sleep margins from
100ms to 500ms. The 50ms debounce with 100ms sleep left only 50ms of
margin, causing timing races on slow Windows CI runners.

commit.message:
paper-audit v1.0 — 7-round adversarial audit framework for academic papers

Agent-driven methodology: 11 specialized agents across 7 sequential rounds
(factual foundation → internal consistency → cross-paper coherence → figures
+ reproducibility → writing quality → bibliography + claims → final integration).

Includes sanitized real-world example: 232 findings across 3 ML papers,
87 fixed, zero CRITICAL/MAJOR remaining at completion.

MPL-2.0 licensed. Domain-agnostic framework, ML/AI-tuned agents.

TASUMER MAF — examine every default.

commit.message:
Add stream-json live agent visibility for autopilot (#132)

Run autopilot agents with --output-format stream-json --verbose to get
real-time event streaming. Parse the stream to show live tool calls,
turn counts, and cost in the TUI slot display.

- Add scanner goroutine that reads stdout pipe, writes raw JSONL to log,
  and updates per-slot LiveStatus (tool, turns, cost)
- Change runAgent() from cmd.Run() to cmd.Start()+StdoutPipe()+cmd.Wait()
- Extend SlotInfo with CurrentTool, TurnCount, MaxTurns, CostUSD, MaxBudget
- Two-line slot display: issue/elapsed/turns/cost + current tool with input
- Update log viewer to parse real stream-json format (nested assistant
  messages, result events with duration_ms/total_cost_usd)
- Enrich StatusBlock() with turn count, cost, and current tool for analyzer

fix(backend): accept all CORS origins — JWT auth makes CORS moot

Desktop app (Tauri) uses https://tauri.localhost or tauri://localhost
as its origin which differs per OS/WebView2 version. Since the API
is secured by JWT (Bearer token), CORS restriction provides no
additional security. Use origin:true to reflect any origin.

fix: dashboard e2e adds auth bypass and fixes broken selectors

- auth.ts: E2E_SKIP_AUTH=1 branch skips NextAuth, returns fake session
- proxy.ts: explicit req type annotation for auth callback
- core-flows.spec.ts: fix invalid CSS selector, soft-assert System Info

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(daily): server-side transcription + LLM cleanup (#261)

* feat(daily): server-side transcription with parakeet-mlx and LLM cleanup (#260)

Add server-side voice note processing pipeline for Parachute Daily:
audio uploads are transcribed via Parakeet V3 on Apple MLX (Metal GPU),
then cleaned up by an auto-triggered Caller for publication-quality text.

Phase 1 — TranscriptionService:
- Backend protocol with platform-specific implementations
- ParakeetMLXBackend for macOS Metal GPU acceleration
- Config fields: transcription_enabled, transcription_model_id
- Wired into server startup + bot connector via InterfaceRegistry
- parakeet-mlx dependency (macOS-only) + FFmpeg install check

Phase 2 — Voice entry endpoint:
- POST /api/daily/entries/voice accepts multipart audio upload
- Background task: transcribe → update entry → trigger cleanup
- New "transcribed" intermediate status (processing → transcribed → complete)

Phase 3 — Cleanup Caller + tools:
- Built-in "transcription-cleanup" Caller seeded on module load
- update_entry tool for agents to modify Note content in graph
- Auto-triggered after transcription completes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review findings on server transcription PR

P1 fixes:
- Store background task refs in module-level set to prevent GC from
  swallowing exceptions; attach done-callback that logs errors
- Change transcription-cleanup Caller trust_level from "direct" to
  "sandboxed" — it only needs read_journal + update_entry tools
- Add 200 MB file size limit on voice upload (reject before saving)
- Add audio file extension allowlist (.wav, .m4a, .mp3, .aac, .ogg,
  .webm, .mp4); strip original filename, keep only UUID + extension

P2 fixes:
- Move full read-modify-write in _update_entry_transcription_status
  inside write_lock to prevent race conditions with concurrent PATCH
- Combine content + metadata write into single atomic Cypher SET in
  _transcribe_and_cleanup to avoid inconsistent state on partial failure
- Add os.fsync() after tmp.flush() in transcribe_bytes_sync before
  passing temp file to parakeet-mlx/FFmpeg

P3 fixes:
- Add proper type annotations (Settings param, Any return types,
  modern X | None syntax)
- Add shutdown() to ParakeetMLXBackend + TranscriptionService; wire
  into server lifespan teardown
- Clean up orphaned audio files on transcription failure

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: Flutter app server-side transcription integration (#263)

* feat(daily): wire Flutter app to server-side transcription pipeline

Server capability discovery (transcription_available in health endpoint),
TranscriptionMode setting (auto/server/local), voice entry upload via
POST /api/daily/entries/voice, in-flight entry polling, and transcription
status model updates (transcribed intermediate state).

Core flow: when in auto mode and server has transcription, audio uploads
directly to server for Parakeet MLX transcription + LLM cleanup. Falls
back to local transcription on failure. Polling refreshes entries every
5s until transcription completes.

Closes #262

* feat(daily): add transcription settings UI and entry state indicators

New TranscriptionSettingsSection in Settings with mode selector
(Auto/Server/Local), server availability status chip, and visual
option cards. Updated JournalEntryCard to show server-side states:
processing shimmer, cleanup-in-progress badge, and failed indicator.

* docs: mark acceptance criteria complete for #262

* fix: address review findings for server transcription integration

P1: Fix health.py transcription_available always returning False — was
checking request.app.state.transcribe_audio (nonexistent), now uses
get_registry().get("TranscriptionService").

P1: Fix polling timer leak — replace uncancellable Future.delayed with
per-entry Timer map, cancel all in dispose(). Add re-entry guard to
prevent duplicate timeouts on journal reload.

P2: Unify stringly-typed serverTranscriptionStatus (String?) with
TranscriptionStatus enum — add 'processing' value, change field type,
update all comparisons and parsing.

P2: Inline _shouldUseServerTranscription, read transcriptionModeProvider
once at top of _addVoiceEntry to avoid double-read in fallback path.

P2: Fix uploadVoiceEntry field mapping — server returns 'entry_id' not
'id', and 'audio_path' at top level not nested in metadata.

P3: Merge duplicate indicator widgets into _buildServerStatusIndicator,
remove unused _ModeOption.mode parameter.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: voice entry type mismatch, simplify cleanup, fix polling

- Server stored type "audio" but Flutter expected "voice" — entries
  rendered as plain text instead of voice notes. Fixed server to use
  "voice" and added "audio" as fallthrough alias in Flutter.

- Replaced heavyweight cleanup Caller (Docker sandbox + agent loop +
  tool use) with a direct single-turn Haiku SDK call. Text in, cleaned
  text out — no tools, no sandbox needed.

- Fixed polling to update UI whenever content changes (raw text appears
  immediately) while continuing to poll until terminal state (so cleaned
  text replaces raw text a few seconds later).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Show banner in Design Studio when no base map exists

Displays a subtle prompt linking to Boundary Map when baseMap is null.

commit.message:
Iris/CW: namespace-qualify RBAC and isolate canary lifecycle (#3703)

## Summary

Fixes #3698 — multiple Iris instances on the same CKS cluster no longer
interfere with each other's lifecycle.

**Problem:** ClusterRole/ClusterRoleBinding were hardcoded to
`iris-controller` (cluster-scoped), so tearing down one Iris namespace
destroyed RBAC for all others. The canary workflow also ran in the
default `iris` namespace, so its nightly teardown would kill any
persistent workloads there.

**Fix:**
1. Qualify cluster-scoped RBAC names with namespace
(`iris-controller-{ns}`)
2. Move the canary to its own namespace (`iris-canary`) via a dedicated
config, freeing `iris` for persistent use

## What changed

- **`coreweave.py`** — `ensure_rbac()` creates
`iris-controller-{namespace}` ClusterRole/Binding; `stop_controller()`
cleans them up
- **`coreweave-canary.yaml`** (new) — canary-specific config with
`namespace: iris-canary`, `label_prefix: iris-canary`, separate
`remote_state_dir`
- **`marin-canary-ferry-cw.yaml`** — points at `coreweave-canary.yaml`;
concurrency group, teardown label, and diagnostics namespace all scoped
to `iris-canary`
- **`coreweave.md`** — RBAC table updated

<details>
<summary>Why key on namespace, not label_prefix?</summary>

RBAC binds a ServiceAccount (namespaced) to a ClusterRole. Keying on
namespace keeps the naming aligned with K8s's own isolation boundary.
Two configs with the same namespace but different `label_prefix` should
share one ClusterRole, not create two that grant the same SA identical
permissions.
</details>

<details>
<summary>What already worked (no changes needed)</summary>

- **NodePool isolation** — already uses `label_prefix` for names and
labels
- **Namespaced resources** (SA, Deployment, Service, ConfigMap, Secret)
— already isolated by namespace
- **GCP platform** — doesn't use K8s RBAC
</details>

## Test plan

- [x] Existing `test_coreweave_platform.py` suite passes (47 tests)
- [x] New `test_rbac_isolation_across_namespaces` — two platforms
create/teardown non-overlapping RBAC
- [x] `./infra/pre-commit.py --all-files --fix` passes
- [x] **Manual on CW** — verified end-to-end ([run
23119639900](https://github.com/marin-community/marin/actions/runs/23119639900))

<details>
<summary>Manual test details</summary>

Started default cluster (`namespace: iris`) from laptop, then triggered
the canary workflow on this branch with `target_tokens=65536`.

**During canary run:** both ClusterRoles coexisted:
```
iris-controller-iris          2026-03-15T20:52:22Z
iris-controller-iris-canary   2026-03-15T21:29:19Z
```

**After canary teardown:**
| Check | Result |
|---|---|
| `iris-controller-iris` ClusterRole | Still exists |
| Default controller pod (`iris` namespace) | 1/1 Running |
| `iris-controller-iris-canary` ClusterRole | Gone (cleaned up) |
| Canary NodePools (`iris-canary-*`) | Gone (cleaned up) |
| Default NodePools (`iris-*`) | Unaffected |

The canary ferry itself failed at the training step (unrelated — likely
65k token edge case), but `Start CoreWeave cluster`, `Submit canary
ferry`, and `Tear down CoreWeave cluster` all succeeded, which is what
matters for the isolation test.

Note: a stale `iris-controller` ClusterRole from a pre-PR run (Feb 19)
was found on the cluster — confirms the problem this PR fixes.
</details>

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: remove duplicate CORS headers - gateway handles CORS

- auth-service: remove @CrossOrigin from AuthController
- event-service: disable CORS in SecurityConfig (.cors(cors -> cors.disable()))

CORS headers were being sent twice (once by gateway, once by service),
causing browser to reject requests with "multiple values" error.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: batch Gemini calls across qualification, insights, and evaluation

Extends batch API usage beyond evaluation to all batchable Gemini calls:

Qualification: refactored scanner to separate crawl (Step B) from LLM
classification (Step C). All ambiguous businesses now have their LLM
classifier prompts collected and submitted as one batch_generate() call
instead of N individual calls.

Insights: added generate_insights_batch() that collects prompts for all
analyzed businesses and submits them as one batch. Moved from per-business
(inside Cloud Task) to post-analysis batch (in engine). Removes per-business
generate_insights() call from _run_workflow_analyze.

gemini_batch.py: added batch_generate() for inline batching with semaphore-
controlled concurrency (3 concurrent). Renamed old function to
submit_vertex_batch() for large batch jobs.

Non-batchable agents (SEO auditor, competitive profiler, discovery scanner)
still use tools and run via ADK — these cannot be batched.

commit.message:
Add orWhereNull, orWhereNotNull, orWhereBetween, orWhereNotBetween, orWhereLike, orWhereNotLike convenience methods (#164)

## What this change does

Adds six new convenience methods to `SelectBuilder` that are the natural
OR-condition companions to the existing `whereNull`, `whereNotNull`,
`whereBetween`, `whereNotBetween`, `whereLike`, and `whereNotLike`
methods:

| New method | OR-equivalent of |
|---|---|
| `orWhereNull(column)` | `whereNull(column)` |
| `orWhereNotNull(column)` | `whereNotNull(column)` |
| `orWhereBetween(column, range)` | `whereBetween(column, range)` |
| `orWhereNotBetween(column, range)` | `whereNotBetween(column, range)`
|
| `orWhereLike(column, pattern)` | `whereLike(column, pattern)` |
| `orWhereNotLike(column, pattern)` | `whereNotLike(column, pattern)` |

Each method is a one-liner that delegates to `orWhere()`, keeping the
implementation minimal and consistent with the existing pattern.

## Example usage

```typescript
// Find users who are active OR whose account has not been deleted
qb.select('users')
  .where('active = ?', true)
  .orWhereNull('deleted_at')
  .execute()
// SELECT * FROM users WHERE (active = ?) OR (deleted_at IS NULL)

// Find products in a featured category OR in the $10–$100 price range
qb.select('products')
  .where('featured = ?', true)
  .orWhereBetween('price', [10, 100])
  .execute()
// SELECT * FROM products WHERE (featured = ?) OR (price BETWEEN ? AND ?)

// Search by name or email
qb.select('users')
  .whereLike('name', '%john%')
  .orWhereLike('email', '%john%')
  .execute()
// SELECT * FROM users WHERE (name LIKE ?) OR (email LIKE ?)
```

## Why it's beneficial

The existing
`whereNull`/`whereNotNull`/`whereBetween`/`whereNotBetween`/`whereLike`/`whereNotLike`
methods make common SQL patterns more readable. Without their `orWhere`
counterparts, users have to write raw `orWhere('column IS NULL')`
strings, losing the ergonomic benefit of the convenience API. Adding
these six methods completes the symmetry.

## Tests

Nine new unit tests added to `tests/unit/new-features.test.ts` covering:
- Each new method producing the correct SQL and parameters
- The "no prior where" case (equivalent to the non-OR version)
- Combined usage with existing `where()` calls

All 302 existing tests continue to pass.

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
